Mineral Availability: Low to moderate uranium and thorium deposits; uraninite veins in Kulu-Banjar, Himachal Pradesh; Precambrian metasediments, selective granites
Deposit Distance: Local veins/intrusions often within 10-50km radius of region; surface & shallow depths in quartzite and granite
Type/Form: Uraninite vein fillings, minor zircon and monazite grains
Geological Notes: Low T-P mineralisation, tensional folding, fracture zones, Himalaya tectonics

Mineral Availability: Trace uranium and thorium, mainly secondary minerals (autunite, torbernite, carnotite); Kern River Canyon and Mojave Desert
Deposit Distance: Prospects found 10-100km from major rivers and granite belts; mostly near-surface
Type/Form: Secondary uranium minerals in fractures; thorium in granites
Geological Notes: Granodiorite, weathered granite, minor volcanic host

Mineral Availability: High concentrations (Udmaru/Nubra-Shyok valley granite dyke); Uranium up to 5.36% in zircon situ; thorium-rich granite dykes
Deposit Distance: Precise deposit at Udmaru village, near surface granite within tens of km of valley
Type/Form: Greenish zircon, thorite, uraninite in granite dykes
Geological Notes: Volcanic rock formation, Nubra-Shyok valley, isotope anomalies

Mineral Availability: Sandstone and granite-related uranium deposits, epigenetic alteration, Ordos Basin
Deposit Distance: Deposits across Ordos, proximal sandstone layers in Huanhe Formation
Type/Form: Coffinite, titanium–uranium oxides in fissures, pyrite and biotite matrix
Geological Notes: Intense epigenetic alteration, proximal debris, redox barriers

Mineral Availability: Extensive surface and near-surface uranium, especially Yazd Province, granite-hosted, sandstone-hosted, and Cenozoic intrusions
Deposit Distance: Mining centers within 50-250km of Yazd, multiple surface and subsurface prospects
Geological Notes: diverse domains across Tethyan belt, Phanerozoic sedimentary cover

Mineral Availability: Coffinite, metaschoepite, xenotime, thorite, monazite; pegmatites, surface deposits, and clay alteration zones
Deposit Distance: Pegmatite fields local within 10–40km of regional mining towns; depth a few meters to outcrop
Type/Form: Pegmatite, clay-altered feldspar with uranium and thorium oxides
Geological Notes: Arid climate, ancient granites, enriched radioelements, N-S trending mineral belt

Mineral Availability: Very minor uranium, mainly accessory minerals in granitic rocks
Deposit Distance: Widespread in Sierra granite, scattered
Geological Notes: Sierra granites, major batholith, minor geochemical anomalies

Mineral Availability: Low-grade uraniferous volcanic tuffs; uranium as powdery uraniferous opal layers; some thorium trace minerals
Deposit Distance: Tuff beds and volcanic flows are local (Virgin Valley, central Nevada); ore layers a few feet thick, up to half a mile
Geological Notes: Volcanic ash, basalt flows, hot dry climate, isolated repository location

Mineral Availability: Graphitic phyllites contain uranium (mean ~4.9 ppm) and thorium (~22 ppm); limited granites, gneiss also contain traces
Deposit Distance: Ore and mineral traces in phyllite zones, 10-50km from mountain core; often surface accessible
Type/Form: Phyllite-hosted uranium, thorium in granite gneiss
Geological Notes: Antiform structure, complex folding, Blue Ridge province rocks

Mineral Availability: Albitite ore bodies, tabular lenses, lenticular stocks; grades ~0.1–0.2% U; stratabound sandstone type (0.02–0.06% U) widespread
Deposit Distance: Ore bodies within 10–100km of major regional faults; 3–50m thick, 100–200m under cover rocks
Geological Notes: Regionally metasomatized, high tectonic activity, mineralization at intersection of faults

Mineral Availability: Major deposits in Fen field (Telemark); thorium found in monazite, bastnaesite, thorite; uranium also present
Deposit Distance: Main mineral field of 2,500 acres, Thorium Norway operations in Fen complex; shallow, mapped by radioactivity
Geological Notes: Ancient carbonatite, rare earth enriched, prospective commercial exploitation

Mineral Availability: Minor uraniferous tuff and volcanic flows, scattered occurrences
Deposit Distance: Prospects spread widely, volcanic province
Geological Notes: Hot, dry, historic uranium exploration

Mineral Availability: Accessory uranium and thorium in granites, secondary minerals
Deposit Distance: Widespread in Sierra granite, Mojave Desert, Kern Canyon
Geological Notes: Granodiorite, weathered granite, minor volcanic host

Mineral Availability: Graphitic phyllites, granite and gneiss hosts; ppm levels
Deposit Distance: Northern mountains, scattered across state
Geological Notes: Complex folding, tectonic provinces

Mineral Availability: Albitite ore bodies, tabular lenses, grades ~0.1–0.2% U
Deposit Distance: Craton-wide, granitic host, Kirovohrad
Geological Notes: Precambrian shield, active mining area

Mineral Availability: Strata-bound quartz-pebble conglomerate (QPC) bearing low-to-moderate detrital uranium (1.8–3.1 ppm soil, trace uraninite detritus)
Deposit Distance: QPC zone near Kalasapura, ~34 km strike length, near-surface
Type/Form: Detrital uraninite in QPC, Proterozoic sedimentary horizon
Geological Notes: Bababudan Group base, Dharwar Supergroup, ancient Archaean basement, undeveloped soils

Mineral Availability: Vein-type, sandstone-hosted uranium (Ghateshwar, Rohil); low to medium grade
Deposit Distance: Localized in Proterozoic basins and E-W trending faults
Type/Form: Pitchblende, coffinite, uraninite in carbonaceous matter
Geological Notes: Vein-type in sheared basement granite and tectonized limestone, Proterozoic origin

Mineral Availability: Sandstone and volcanic-hosted uranium; deep and shallow prospects known
Deposit Distance: Explored regionally in sandstone strata, several km long
Geological Notes: Arid Gansu corridor, deep exploration, Ordos/Tarim basin vicinity

Mineral Availability: World’s deepest industrial sandstone-type uranium at depth (Tarim Basin, ~1,820m); large and thick-bedded discovery
Deposit Distance: Deep (1.8km+) and also shallow sandstones in Junggar area
Geological Notes: Red variegated strata, frontier desert exploration, deep drilling required

Mineral Availability: Moderate uranium occurrences in Precambrian igneous-volcanic association (dolerite, tholeiitic basalt, rhyolite); accessory uraninite
Deposit Distance: Outcrop area in Punjab plains, locally exposed
Geological Notes: Kirana-Malani basin remnant volcanics, meta-sedimentary contacts

Mineral Availability: Region characterized by granites, conglomerate, limestone, chromite, diorite; no significant uranium exploitation but suitable for underground labs
Deposit Distance: Extensive granite range (140 mi), desert and mountain terrain
Geological Notes: Nuclear test site, remote and arid, few resources developed

Mineral Availability: Surface and alluvial sand deposits, not notable for uranium/thorium
Deposit Distance: Alluvial sands widespread, near-surface
Geological Notes: Desert plain, cross-border with India, alluvial regimes dominate

Mineral Availability: Extensive roll-front uranium deposits in Tertiary sandstones (south Texas, Panhandle); In-situ recovery common
Deposit Distance: Minerals in sandstones within 100–200m depth, major fields in coastal plain
Geological Notes: Gulf coastal plain, Cenozoic sandstones, leading US uranium production

Mineral Availability: Granitic rocks with uranium and thorium anomalies, monazite, euxenite
Deposit Distance: Shield zone, outcrops regionally in Hijaz/Asir, mapped by radiometrics
Geological Notes: Proterozoic Arabian-Nubian shield, ancient granites, arid exposure

Mineral Availability: Phosphorite beds with uranium enrichment (Rotem Plateau); minor secondary deposits
Deposit Distance: Phosphorites widespread in southern desert, near-surface
Geological Notes: Rotem, Zin Valley, Cretaceous/Tertiary beds, semiarid

Mineral Availability: Stratiform sedimentary uranium, now mostly exhausted, formerly mined in Jurassic limestones and marls
Deposit Distance: Uranium in shallow Jurassic beds, near paleochannels
Geological Notes: Sedimentary basin, former uranium mining region, depleted

Mineral Availability: Clay rock (argillite) host for nuclear waste, not an ore field
Deposit Distance: Underground host, 500m depth
Geological Notes: Selected for geological disposal, stable, low-permeability substrate

Location: Rio de la Plata Craton, Brazil
Mineral Availability: Metasomatized albitite and granitic rocks with uranium minerals; rare earths
Deposit Distance: Outcrop and subsurface zones across the craton
Geological Notes: Neoproterozoic craton, stable, uranium resource province

Mineral Availability: Secondary uranium in sandstones, granites, rare earth elements; minor thorium
Deposit Distance: Basins and granites with surface or shallow resources
Geological Notes: Precambrian basement, Amazonas basin, stable but little developed

Mineral Availability: World-class volcanogenic uranium deposits, high-grade uraninite, lithium in tuff; recent exploration
Deposit Distance: Plateau surface, tuff outcrops and shallow pits
Type/Form: Uraninite in volcanic tuff, lithium minerals
Geological Notes: Volcanic plateau, recent prospecting, unique high-Li and U combo

Mineral Availability: Known for brine and evaporite (lithium, boron); uranium only as trace
Deposit Distance: Evaporites on plateau, near-surface
Geological Notes: High Andean plateau, extreme environment, major astronomy site
